strengths: Mind-map clearly mapping ATL skills to the learning goal with detailed evidence of self-management and research skills
Discussion of media interpretations demonstrating critical awareness of source bias and informed source selection
Diagram linking ATL skills to the product goal, showing use of CodePen and perseverance in the creation process
Reflection on the synergy of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript illustrating effective application of technology skills
Demonstrated persistence in debugging, ensuring a functioning, interactive website
OPVL table providing strong evidence of thorough source evaluation and data verification
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You excel at applying ATL skills: each skill is not only named but illustrated with relevant examples and evidence that show real impact on both your learning and the quality of the website. The detailed mind-maps, OPVL analysis, and reflections on coding perseverance fully satisfy the highest expectations.

strengths: Clearly stated a focused learning goal connected to personal interest in highlighting women in STEM
Effectively linked the product goal (educational website) to the learning goal and target audience
Well-defined intended product with justification of its educational and inspirational impact
Outlined concrete resources and strategies (tutorials, weekly hours, feedback) for skill development
Provided a detailed, step-by-step action plan with time allocations demonstrating feasibility
Set measurable criteria to track website progress through sections, layout techniques, interactive features, and user testing
weaknesses: Success criteria table is incomplete; needs specific criteria, justifications, and planned testing methods
Calendar visual lacks task labels or color-coding to clarify sequencing and responsibilities
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning is strong: you articulate a clear learning goal rooted in personal interest, define a relevant product with well-justified impact, and outline concrete strategies and a detailed timeline. The action plan and measurable criteria demonstrate feasibility and focus. To fully meet the rubric, complete the success criteria table with specific measures and testing methods, and enhance the calendar graphic by labeling tasks or using color-coding to show the sequence of activities.

strengths: Concluding improvement plan succinctly summarises refinements for aesthetics, navigation, scalability, content completeness, and interactivity
Insightful reflection on becoming more self-directed and developing perseverance through the research process
Deep reflection linking data collection, recording, and verification skills to the learning goal and resolving source contradictions
Clear evidence of improved time management through the use of a weekly planner supporting thorough research
Thorough evaluation of the product against detailed success criteria with justifications and specific examples
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflections are comprehensive and insightful: you evaluate the product against each success criterion with concrete evidence, and you articulate how the process has impacted your research approach, self-management, and technical skills. The improvement plan demonstrates forward-looking thinking. No major gaps are evident in your reflective work.

annotations.comment: You clearly state a focused learning goal and convincingly link it to your personal interest in highlighting women in STEM, demonstrating strong engagement and purpose for your research.
You effectively link the product goal to your learning goal by targeting a younger generation online, showing strong relevance.
The concluding improvement plan succinctly summarises key refinements for aesthetics, navigation, scalability, content completeness, and interactivity (C(ii)).
Your reflection on being self-directed shows insightful understanding of your growth in planning and perseverance through your first independent research project (C(i)).
Your intended product is well definedâa website using HTML, CSS, and JavaScriptâand you justify its relevance with clear rationale on its educational and inspirational impact.
Your achievable strategies outline concrete resources (tutorials, weekly hours, action plan, feedback) to develop your coding and research skills.
The product success criteria table is empty. Populate it with specific criteria, justifications and planned testing methods to fully meet A(ii) expectations.
Your step-by-step action plan with time allocations and completed times demonstrates a detailed, feasible plan for product development (A(iii)).
The calendar visual lacks task labels. Add task names or color-coding to each date to show sequencing of your plan and improve clarity.
Your measurable criteria clearly define how you will track website progress (e.g., completing sections, layout techniques, interactive features, user testing).
This mind-map diagram clearly maps ATL skills to your learning goal with detailed evidence of self-management and research skills (B(i)).
Your discussion of media interpretations demonstrates critical awareness of source bias and how it informed your selection of reliable information (B1).
The diagram linking ATL skills to your product goal is clear and shows how using Codepen and perseverance supported the creation process (B(ii)).
Your reflection on understanding how HTML, CSS and JavaScript function in harmony shows effective application of technology skills to enhance the product (B2).
You illustrate persistence in debugging common coding errors, reflecting genuine perseverance that ensured a functioning, interactive website (B(ii)).
You link âcollect, record and verify dataâ directly to your learning goal and reflect on resolving contradictions between sources, showing deep impact on your research approach (C(i)).
The OPVL table provides strong evidence of collecting, recording and verifying data, demonstrating thorough evaluation of your sources (B(i)).
Your explanation of how using a weekly planner improved time management and supported thorough research demonstrates clear impact on your learning process (C(i)).
Your evaluation table compares the product against detailed success criteria with justifications and evidence, showing a thorough reflection on product quality (C(ii)).
